summary: dotenv typosquat with postinstall credential and crypto-wallet stealer
details: This package typosquats the widely used `dotenv` package (double-v). Its `index.js` is a dummy stub that falsely labels itself as a lodash utility bundle, while the real payload lives in `postinstall.js`, which runs silently on `npm install`. The postinstall script reads `~/.npmrc`, `~/.env`, and `~/.git-credentials`, regex-extracts npm tokens (`npm_[a-zA-Z0-9]{36}`), `AWS_ACCESS_KEY_ID` / `AWS_SECRET_ACCESS_KEY`, `GITHUB_TOKEN`, and the full git credential store. It additionally enumerates 71 browser crypto-wallet extension IDs (MetaMask, Phantom, Coinbase, Trust, Ledger, Trezor, etc.) across Chrome, Brave, Edge, Chromium, Vivaldi, and Opera profile directories, reads their Local Extension Settings `.log` files, scrapes `vault`, `mnemonic`, `privateKey`, `seed`, and `password` patterns, and scans the user's Documents/Desktop/Downloads for BIP-39 seed-phrase files. Collected data, along with hostname and username, is POSTed to a hardcoded bare-IP C2 at `http://149.28.127.35:8888`. The file header explicitly self-describes as `Token harvester + Crypto wallet scanner... Silent. Zero trace.`

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
